title: Dasystephana decora
taxonomy: Dasystephana decora is a species of flowering plant in the family Asteraceae. It is native to the island of Réunion in the Indian Ocean. The classification of Dasystephana decora includes its kingdom as Plantae and its order as Asterales. The plant is also classified as a member of the genus Dasystephana. Further classification includes its family as Asteraceae and its kingdom as Plantae.

intro: Dasystephana decora, also known as the decorated mistflower, is a flowering plant species in the family Asteraceae. This plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine for its various health benefits, including anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties, which can help alleviate symptoms of fever, sore throats, and respiratory issues. The bioactive constituents of Dasystephana decora include flavonoids, terpenoids, and phenolic acids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made from Dasystephana decora include teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are often used to treat various health conditions, including colds, coughs, and skin irritations.
